name: GPT-5.5
description: OpenAI's flagship from April 2026 (codename 'Spud'), first fully retrained base model since GPT-4.5. Succeeded at the top of the lineup by the GPT-5.6 family (Sol/Terra/Luna, publicly released 2026-07-09) but fully supported with no announced shutdown, and still the designated migration target for most of the GPT-5.x line. ~1.05M context, 85.0% ARC-AGI-2, 93.6% GPQA Diamond, 58.6% SWE-Bench Pro.

evidence.value: 58.6% on SWE-Bench Pro (state-of-the-art at release)
82.7% on command-line agentic tasks

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Industry-standard coding and terminal benchmarks measuring real-world software engineering tasks

evidence.value: 93.6% (PhD-level science questions)
85.0% (large jump over GPT-5.2's 52.9%, industry-leading abstract reasoning)

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: PhD-level science, frontier mathematics, and abstract reasoning benchmarks

evidence.value: 84.9% on economically valuable knowledge-work tasks
78.7% on computer-use tasks (improving on GPT-5.4's 75%)

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Expert-comparison knowledge work and computer-use benchmarks

evidence.value: First fully retrained base since GPT-4.5; ~40% fewer output tokens than GPT-5.4 for equivalent quality

output_consistency.methodology: Internal consistency testing reported by provider across reasoning effort levels

evidence.value: Token efficiency gains (~40% fewer output tokens) reduce end-to-end response times vs GPT-5.4

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 latency varies significantly with reasoning effort setting

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: ~1.05M token input context, 128K max output tokens
99.9% uptime (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: First fully retrained base since GPT-4.5. State-of-the-art across reasoning (85.0% ARC-AGI-2, 93.6% GPQA) and agentic coding (82.7% Terminal-Bench 2.0). ~40% more token-efficient than GPT-5.4.

evidence.value: Strengthened injection defenses for agentic and computer-use workflows

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection attacks

evidence.value: Retrained base with updated safety training; improved refusal robustness over GPT-5.4

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Adversarial prompt testing against jailbreak datasets

evidence.value: No training on API data by default

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Multi-layer safety stack carried forward and recalibrated for the retrained base

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key + OAuth2 authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Mature multi-layer safety stack. Retrained base required full safety recalibration, which OpenAI reports as complete; long-tail agentic behaviors still being characterized by third parties.

evidence.value: Data residency options for enterprise customers
API data not used for training by default

training_data_optout.methodology: Policy review of data usage terms

evidence.value: 30-day default API log retention; zero-data-retention options for qualifying customers

data_retention.methodology: Terms of service and enterprise documentation review

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; moderation API available
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR compliant
Zero-data-retention options available for enterprise and qualifying API customers

privacy_compliance.notes: Standard OpenAI enterprise posture: SOC 2, no API-data training by default, 30-day default retention with zero-data-retention options.

evidence.value: Adjustable reasoning effort with visible reasoning summaries

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: Retrained base continues factuality gains; builds on GPT-5.4's ~33% factual-error reduction

hallucination_rate.methodology: Factual accuracy testing on QA datasets

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and red-teaming program
Improved calibrated uncertainty expression with reduced confident errors

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Detailed release documentation covering capabilities, benchmarks, and migration guidance
General description of training approach; specific sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Multi-layer safety guardrails with agentic-workflow protections

trust_transparency.notes: Strong transparency with reasoning summaries and detailed release documentation. Training data disclosure remains at industry-standard (limited) level.

evidence.value: Responses API with streaming, function calling, vision, computer use, reasoning effort control

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs for Python, Node.js, Go, .NET, actively maintained

sdk_quality.methodology: SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance review

evidence.value: Published deprecation schedule; GPT-5.5 is the designated migration target for most of the GPT-5.x line
Re-verified: GPT-5.5 remains the named replacement for gpt-5, gpt-5.1, gpt-5.2-chat-latest, gpt-5.2-codex, and gpt-5.3-chat-latest deprecations; no GPT-5.5 deprecation announced

GPT-5.6 family (Sol/Terra/Luna) previewed 2026-06-26 under U.S. government-requested partner-only restrictions and publicly released 2026-07-09; Sol priced $5/$30, Terra $2.50/$15, Luna $1/$6 per 1M tokens

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical deprecation practices

evidence.value: Detailed usage dashboard with costs, tokens, rate limits

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: 24/7 support, comprehensive docs, active developer community
Largest AI ecosystem; available in ChatGPT (2026-04-23) and API (2026-04-24) with Batch and Flex tiers
Standard commercial terms with usage policies

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Industry-leading operational maturity. As the designated GPT-5.x migration target, GPT-5.5 offers a long expected support horizon even after the GPT-5.6 family (Sol/Terra/Luna) launched 2026-07-09.

code-generation.notes: 58.6% SWE-Bench Pro and 82.7% Terminal-Bench 2.0. ~1.05M context fits very large codebases. Codex variants remain preferable for dedicated agentic coding pipelines.

customer-support.notes: Token efficiency (~40% fewer output tokens) lowers cost per conversation. $5/$30 pricing is premium for high-volume support.

content-creation.notes: Retrained base produces concise, higher-quality drafts. Strong long-form coherence over very long contexts.

data-analysis.notes: 93.6% GPQA and 51.7% FrontierMath T1-3 support rigorous quantitative work. ~1.05M context enables whole-dataset reasoning.

research-assistant.notes: 84.9% GDPval on expert knowledge work with ~1.05M context for literature-scale inputs.

legal-compliance.notes: Strong document reasoning; SOC 2 and zero-data-retention options available, but not HIPAA eligible and 30-day default retention.

healthcare.notes: Excellent clinical reasoning (93.6% GPQA) but not HIPAA eligible; privacy controls less strict than Anthropic's.

financial-analysis.notes: Frontier math performance (51.7% FrontierMath T1-3) and GDPval results support complex financial modeling.

education.notes: Top-tier STEM reasoning with adjustable effort for tutoring at different depths. Reduced hallucinations vs prior generations.

creative-writing.notes: Strong narrative quality; conciseness bias from token-efficiency training can need prompting for expansive prose.

strengths: Industry-leading abstract reasoning: 85.0% ARC-AGI-2, 93.6% GPQA Diamond
State-of-the-art agentic coding: 58.6% SWE-Bench Pro, 82.7% Terminal-Bench 2.0
First fully retrained base since GPT-4.5 with ~40% fewer output tokens than GPT-5.4
84.9% GDPval on economically valuable knowledge work
Designated long-term migration target for the GPT-5.x line
limitations: Premium pricing: $5/$30 per 1M tokens (2x GPT-5.4's base rate)
No longer the newest model: GPT-5.6 family released 2026-07-09; GPT-5.6 Terra is reported competitive with GPT-5.5 at half the price ($2.50/$15)
30-day default API data retention (zero retention requires enterprise arrangement)
GPT-5.5 Pro is very expensive ($30/$180 per 1M)
Recently retrained base — long-tail behaviors less battle-tested than GPT-5.x predecessors
best_for: Frontier reasoning and research tasks (ARC-AGI-2, GPQA, FrontierMath leader)
Teams consolidating from older GPT-5.x models onto a long-support flagship
not_recommended_for: Cost-sensitive high-volume inference (use mini/nano tiers or Batch)
Applications requiring zero data retention without enterprise agreements

pricing.notes: Confirmed on official pricing page 2026-07-09. Cached input $0.50 per 1M; Batch at 50% discount ($2.50/$15). GPT-5.5 Pro priced at $30/$180 per 1M tokens.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based; first fully retrained base since GPT-4.5 ('Spud')
